Package de.uka.ilkd.key.rule

Class QueryExpand

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.rule.QueryExpand
All Implemented Interfaces:
BuiltInRule, Rule, HasOrigin
public class QueryExpand extends Object implements BuiltInRule
The QueryExpand rule allows to apply contracts or to symbolically execute a query expression in the logic. It replaces the query expression by a new constant and constructs a box formula in the antecedent 'defining' the constant as the result of a method call. The method call is encoded directly as a method call in the box modality. The query is invoked in a context equal to the container type of the query method.

    getTimeOfQuery(Term t)
     
    boolean
    isApplicable(Goal goal, PosInOccurrence pio)
    Important the correctness of the implementation of this rule relies on the following side-conditions: the query term has no free variables the query term does not occur below an update or a modality If you want to change this you need to adapt the application logic by adding preceding updates in front of the new added formula and/or to take care of free variables when introducing the skolemfunction symbol and when replacing the query term by the skolem function.

  • Method Details

    • apply

      public @NonNull ImmutableList<Goal> apply(Goal goal, Services services, RuleApp ruleApp)
      Description copied from interface: Rule
      the rule is applied on the given goal using the information of rule application.
      Specified by:
      apply in interface Rule
      Parameters:
      goal - the Goal on which to apply ruleApp
      services - the Services with the necessary information about the java programs
      ruleApp - the rule application to be executed
      Returns:
      all open goals below \old(goal.node()), i.e. the goals resulting from the rule application

    • queryEvalTerm

      public Pair<Term,Term> queryEvalTerm(Services services, Term query, LogicVariable[] instVars)
      Creates an invocation of a query in a modal operator and stores the value in a new symbol. This is a utility method, that may also be used by other classes.
      Parameters:
      services -
      query - The query on which the query expand rule is applied
      instVars - If null, then the result of the query can be stored in a constant (e.g. res=query(a)). Otherwise, it is a list of logical variables that can be instantiated (using the rules allLeft, exRight) and therefore the result of the query must be stored by function that depends on instVars (e.g. \forall i; res(i)=query(i)). The list may be empty even if it is not null.
      Returns:
      The formula (!{U}<result=query();>result=res_query) & query()=res_query

    • evaluateQueries

      public Term evaluateQueries(Services services, Term term, boolean positiveContext, boolean allowExpandBelowInstQuantifier)
      Apply "evaluate query" to the queries that occur in term. The query evaluations/expansions are inserted into a copy of term that is returned.
      Parameters:
      services -
      term - A formula that potentially contains queries that should be evaluated/expanded.
      positiveContext - Set false iff the term is in a logically negated context wrt. to the succedent.
      allowExpandBelowInstQuantifier - TODO
      Returns:
      A modified version of the term with inserted "query evalutions".

    • replace

      protected Term replace(Term term, Term with, int[] it, int idx, TermServices services)
      Replaces in a term.
      Parameters:
      term -
      with -
      it - iterator with argument positions. This is the path in the syntax tree of term.
      services - TODO
      Returns:
      Resulting term after replacement.

      public String toString()

    • isApplicable

      public boolean isApplicable(Goal goal, PosInOccurrence pio)
      Important the correctness of the implementation of this rule relies on the following side-conditions:
      • the query term has no free variables
      • the query term does not occur below an update or a modality
      If you want to change this you need to adapt the application logic by adding preceding updates in front of the new added formula and/or to take care of free variables when introducing the skolemfunction symbol and when replacing the query term by the skolem function. The method is equipped with a side-effect that stores the age of the term. This information is useful for QueryExpandCost.
      Specified by:
      isApplicable in interface BuiltInRule

    • getTimeOfQuery

      public Long getTimeOfQuery(Term t)
